analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet celebrates "voices of justice" supporting China at the UN and claims a few Western countries failed in an attempt to smear China over Hong Kong and Xinjiang, citing MFA remarks. Evaluation: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) — Yes. The tweet frames Western countries as maliciously smearing China, depicting them negatively and hostile toward China. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) — Partially implied via "voices of justice" and friendly countries' support, but the tweet does not explicitly portray China's international benevolence or cooperative initiatives; focus is defensive rather than presenting China as a global benefactor. So not sufficiently supported. CH3 (China has an epic history) — No historical framing. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values) — Implicit moral high ground is suggested, but there is no explicit advocacy of China’s political system or domestic values; thus not supported. CH5 (CCP government succeeds) — No direct claims about domestic development or governance success. CH6 (Culture/nature/heritage) — Not relevant. Conclusion: Only CH1 is directly promoted.
